hypothesis_en: Mr President, Mr President-in-Office of the Council, Commissioner, Commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, we are here today for the almost ritual meeting to discuss the state of the Lisbon strategy for the Spring Summit. But this catalogue of good intentions has to be concretized, because I believe that in all our languages there is a common aphorism and that hell is paved with good intentions and it is not enough to proclaim them and repeat them every year, but today we have seen, especially from the Commission, a critical analysis of how I would like to refer in an election year to some issues that concern us in particular. It is good to talk about the internal market and financial services, but we must not forget that we have to overcome the blockage on the imposition of capital income and on unacceptable monopoly situations. I would like to ask the President-in-Office of the Council whether he is aware of the fact that the Council of Ministers of the European Communities has adopted a resolution on the situation in the former Yugoslavia. The majority line that was set out in our institution before the Convention, that the entry into force of the Economic and Monetary Union needed a strengthening of economic governance, the Commissioner has referred to this point. It also accepts that what the governments opposed in the Convention, now there is a change of opinion in view of the need to strengthen economic union, can be translated into a rectification, a wise rectification and that the Constitution, the draft Constitution, is amended in this sense. I think that Parliament would have no difficulty in supporting this line clearly, because it makes no sense that we are talking about a Minister of Foreign Affairs and we do not have a Minister of Economy. The Irish model, the Dutch model, the Danish model or the Swedish model clearly demonstrate that it is possible to have a high degree of competitiveness, an effective economy and at the same time a high level of protection. This is an important point, which is and has been referred to by the Commission a little bit at a time, which is the concrete financial perspectives that the Irish Presidency also has to start its debate on, and this is a very important point, because we also find that at that Berlin summit, after a letter that was written, it is said that we want to do many more things. Here, the Commission, fulfilling its responsibility, has made a proposal, perhaps too tailored, of 1 of gross domestic income and we think and Commissioner Barnier presented it to the Council. But you cannot criticize the lack of investment in basic research, and I say this when in my country, in France or in other countries of the European Union, the researchers are demonstrating, not just the workers, the researchers, because we are going down absolutely, you cannot say that we have to investigate more and then reduce the possibilities. Commissioner Buzkin must have our full support. Finally, Mr President, there are three other issues which I would like to mention. The first is that we still trust the Irish Presidency and hope that it will be able to make a serious proposal in March to advance the Constitution. We believe that the agreement reached in Cyprus must be supported. And finally, Mr President, also today, when we have the visit of the Iranian Nobel Prize winner to the European Parliament, we must also maintain our position of support for democracy in Iran and of criticism of the development of the recent elections in this country.
reference_en: thank you (1.51) President President office of Council Commissioners (1.97) ladies and gentlemen (3.72) well this is almost an annual ritual we come back every year to discuss the Lisbon strategy the state of play with the Lisbon strategy in the run-up to the spring summit let's not forget our principle it's a European social (1.14) model a economic strategy based on solidarity and sustainable development but this catalogue of good intentions has to be fleshed out it's got to be implemented and I think in all (1.19) and I think in all our languages there's a saying that the road to hell is paved with good intentions it's not just a case of re= reiterating these good intentions every year the Commission certainly developed a critical analysis of how the Lisbon strategy has been developing in recent years now this year there are elections so I'd like to refer to some issues that are of special concern it's all very well to talk about financial services in the internal market but don't forget that we've got to to clear the blockage on capital tax and unacceptable monopoly situations it's all very well to talk about developing services but it's also important that we have got to address concerns of increasing delocalization of companies and the need for a proper industrial strategy in Europe therefore (1.12) we m= have to be able to develop (1.1) ideas for the implementation of the Lisbon strategy and Mr President by your leave I'd like to refer to current events (2.58) in our group and more broadly in the Parliament where it's probably a majority line (1.09) that the the entry into force of Economic and Monetary Union required beefed up economic governance the Commissioner referred to this point (2.76) m= (1.07) there was a meeting in Berlin which received a fair bit of media coverage and our leaders there proposed there should be a Supercommissioner in charge of the economy well this is an issue of quite some importance that means (1.32) accepting the community method (1.51) it also means accepting that that which governments opposed in the Convention is something they've changed their minds about they re= they realise that there is a need to strengthen Economic and Monetary Union so hopefully you know (1.81) there will be a change of views on the draft Constitution in that respect people often change their minds and I'm certain the Parliament will support it it doesn't make much sense for us to talk about a Foreign Affairs Minister and not have a Foreign Affairs Minister I'm sure Commissioner Solbes will have no problem at all in supporting such a proposal and indeed in defending it before his colleagues in the Commission there's another important point reform shouldn't just mean dismantling social progress achieved by the welfare state the Irish the Dutch the Swedish and the Danish models all show that you can be highly competitive have a a flourishing economy and a high level of social protection so the road to reform must surely entail learning from these models reform does not necessarily mean dismantling the principles and ideas that've made Europe strong a further important point Mr President (1.57) it's one the Commission mentioned almost in passing and that is the financial perspectives the Irish presidency must initiate the debate on this this is of course a critical point because (1.96) in the Berlin Summit which followed on a letter (1.18) there's talk about doing a lot more Lisbon strategy enlargement and so on and so forth we wanna do more but with less (1.44) and this is becoming a matter of faith it would require a miracle which is surely not a a principle to guide one's economic policies on the Commission has made a proposal i= it's a bit tight one point two per cent of gross income (2.47) and this w= and I think that's the minimum you can talk about Commissioner Barnier talked about this you can't you can't be critical about a lack of investment in research w= and I say this because in my own country and in France researchers (1.03) are out on the streets demonstrating it's not just workers because we've cu= because of cuts you can't say we want more research and then cut the budget so the plan proposed by the European Commission (1.32) drawn up by Com= Commissioner Busquin should enjoy our full support finally and I will conclude Mr President three mo= further points bullet points if you will number one we continue to have every faith in the Irish presidency and we feel confident they will be able to come forward with a decent proposal on the Constitution in March secondly we believe that we should support the agreement that was reached in Cyprus that's crucial for the future of the Union and final Mr P= finally Mr President today (1.04) we have the Iranian Nobel Prize winner visiting the Parliament so we must continue to support democracy in Iran and cr= and we must continue to be critical of the way elections have gone in this country recently thank you
source_es: gracias señor Presidente señor Presidente del Consejo señores Comisarios señor Comisario y señora Comisaria Señorías (1.67) venimos hoy a la cita casi ritual para debatir el estado de la estrategia de Lisboa de cara a la Cumbre de primavera conviene recordar nuestros principios que son los de un modelo social europeo renovado basado en valores compartidos y comunes con una estrategia económica de solidaridad y desarrollo sostenible pero este catálogo de buenas intenciones tiene que concretarse porque yo creo que en todos nuestros idiomas hay un aforismo común y es que el infierno está empedrado de buenas intenciones y no basta con proclamarlas y repetirlas todos los años sino que hay hoy hemos visto por parte especialmente de la Comisión un análisis crítico de cómo se está desarrollando la estrategia de Lisboa ~y_ yo querría referirme en un año electoral a algunas cuestiones que nos preocupan especialmente está bien hablar del mercado interior y de los servicios financieros pero no hay que olvidar que tenemos que superar el bloqueo sobre la imposición de las rentas de capital y sobre situaciones de monopolio inaceptables está bien que hablemos del desarrollo de los servicios pero también hay que responder a las preocupaciones sobre la deslocalización creciente y sobre la necesidad de una estrategia industrial en Europa ~y_ por lo tanto tenemos que ser capaces de desarrollar conjuntamente nuestra línea de implementación de la Agenda de Lisboa y me permitirá señor Presidente que haga referencia a acontecimientos actuales el primero es que hemos pensado en nuestro Grupo y en el Parlamento yo creo que es una línea mayoritaria que se concretó en nuestra actitud ante la Convención que la entrada en vigor de la unión económica y monetaria necesitaba un refuerzo de la gobernanza económica el señor Comisario ha hecho referencia a este punto se ha celebrado una de las muchas reuniones esta ha tenido más cobertura de medios en Berlín que hacen nuestros líderes y han propuesto que haya un superministro de Economía en la Comisión bueno este es un tema importante supone que se acepta que sea el método comunitario el que se aplique que eso es absolutamente fundamental se acepta también que aquello a lo que se opusieron los Gobiernos en la Convención ahora hay un cambio de opinión a la vista de que es necesario fortalecer la unión económica bueno eso se puede traducir en un planteamiento de rectificación rectificar es de sabios y que se enmiende la Constitución el proyecto de Constitución en este sentido yo creo que el Parlamento no tendría ninguna dificultad en apoyar esta línea claramente porque no tiene sentido que estemos hablando de un Ministro de Exteriores y no tengamos Ministro de Economía yo creo que el Comisario Solbes no tendrá dificultad en apoyar esta propuesta incluso en defenderla ante la Comisión y luego hay otro aspecto importante cuando se habla de las reformas no se habla o no se debe hablar sólo del desmantelamiento de los avances sociales y del Estado de bienestar el modelo irlandés el modelo holandés el danés o el sueco demuestran fehacientemente que es posible el tener un alto grado de competitividad una ec= economía eficaz y al mismo tiempo un nivel de protección elevado por lo tanto el camino de la reforma tiene que ir siguiendo estos modelos y no es incompatible el reformar con el mantener los principios que han hecho a Europa fuerte y luego señor Presidente también hay otro punto importante que es y ha hecho referencia la Comisión poco de paso a este punto que es la concreción de las próximas perspectivas financieras que le corresponde también a la Presidencia irlandesa iniciar su debate y este es un punto muy importante porque también nos encontramos con que en esa Cumbre de Berlín después de una carta que se escribió se habla de que queremos hacer muchas más cosas estrategia de Lisboa ampliación y demás con mucho menos lo cual ya verdaderamente es una cuestión de fe sería un milagro y no respondería a los principios de la economía más sensata aquí la Comisión cumpliendo con su responsabilidad ha hecho una propuesta quizá demasiado ajustada del uno veinticuatro por ciento de la renta interna bruta y nosotros pensamos y el Parl= y el Comisario Barnier la presentó en el Parlamento que es de lo mínimo que se puede hablar pero no se puede criticar la falta de inversión en investigación básica y lo digo cuando en mi país o en Francia o en otros países de la de la Unión Europea los investigadores se están manifestando no sólo los trabajadores los investigadores porque estamos bajando absolutamente no se puede decir que hay que investigar más y luego reducir absolutamente las posibilidades por eso el plan propuesto por la Comisaria y elaborado por el Comisario Busquin ((applause)) tiene que tener todo nuestro apoyo y por último señor Presidente y concluyo ya hay tres cuestiones más que me gustaría mencionar la primera es que seguimos confiando en la Presidencia irlandesa y esperando que sea capaz de hacer una propuesta en el mes de marzo seria para avanzar en la Constitución que creemos que hay que apoyar el acuerdo que ha intervenido en Chipre creemos que es muy importante para el futuro de la Unión y por último señor Presidente también hoy que tenemos la visita de la Premio Nobel iraní en el Parlamento Europeo también mantener nuestra postura de apoyo a la democracia en Irán y de crítica al desarrollo de las recientes elecciones en este país muchas gracias señor Presidente
